prompt:
Repair `bin/intake-bundle`, an offline support-bundle collector. It accepts `--source DIR --output ARCHIVE` and gathers only the fixed evidence allowlist: application and optional worker logs, the Bash-it version capture, disk facts, and the Bash-it environment configuration. It must redact sensitive assignment values in the collected configuration, record every collected or skipped item in `manifest.tsv`, and publish a byte-for-byte deterministic `.tar.gz` archive without exposing host paths. Missing optional evidence is a recorded skip; missing required evidence is an error that must leave any existing output unchanged. Preserve the existing command line, archive layout, manifest schema, redaction behavior, deterministic metadata, and atomic publication.

The focused defect is in canonical source containment. An allowlisted path may resolve to the source root itself or to a descendant separated by `/`; a similarly named sibling is outside. In particular, if the source is `/case/source`, a symlink resolving under `/case/source-private` must be rejected even though the strings share a prefix. Internal symlinks that resolve beneath the source remain valid. Fix that boundary check so no allowlisted entry can escape through a symlink. Do not modify the protected test. Everything must remain local, offline, and deterministic.

Run the protected regression suite with:

    bash tests/run.sh

runtime_debug.baseline_evidence: Normal collection, redaction, optional skips, required-source failure, internal symlinks, and deterministic archives work. A canonical log path beneath a sibling named with the source-root prefix is nevertheless archived because containment uses a raw string-prefix comparison.
